name: Little Walter
abstract: Marion Walter Jacobs (May 1, 1930 – February 15, 1968), known as Little Walter, was an American blues musician, singer, and songwriter. His revolutionary approach to the harmonica had a strong impact on the succeeding generations, earning him comparisons to such seminal artists as Django Reinhardt, Charlie Parker and Jimi Hendrix.

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison Sr.
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Little Walter. Right from Memphis. Right from under the jug band. That's us in place. All of them. They ain't got nothing on us.

about_quotes.speaker: Will Shade and Dewey Corley
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Little Walter. Right from Memphis. Right from under the jug band. That's us in place. All of them. They ain't got nothing on us.
about_quotes.doc_title: Will Shade and Dewey Corley Interview, 1960-05-25

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I fell in love with the sound of the blues harmonica and all the Chicago guys, you know Paul Butterfield, James Cotton, Little Walter
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 11, 2025
